task_completion.description: Log in to Artifactory as the admin user.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Artifactory web interface is visible in a browser, and the user profile or dashboard indicates an active session for the 'admin' user.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the User Management section in Artifactory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Administration module is open, specifically the 'Identity and Access' -> 'Users' page showing a list of system users.

task_completion.description: Open the profile/settings for the target user 'jmonroe'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Edit User' or user details page specifically for the username 'jmonroe' is displayed.
An 'Unlock' button is clicked, or a 'Locked' status indicator/checkbox is removed or unchecked for the user 'jmonroe', followed by a save or success notification.

The password and confirm password fields are filled (likely with masked dots/asterisks), and the 'Save', 'Update', or 'Change Password' button is clicked. A success toast or message indicating the user was updated should be visible.

task_completion.description: Ensure the user remains a member of the 'backend-devs' group.
task_completion.visual_evidence: On the user edit page for 'jmonroe', the group 'backend-devs' is clearly visible in the 'Assigned Groups' or 'Related Groups' list.

integrity.description: The agent used the web browser GUI to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a web browser (e.g., Chromium/Firefox) navigating the Artifactory UI, rather than terminal windows executing curl commands or API scripts.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual Artifactory application.

integrity.description: The agent did not use browser developer tools to fake success states.
